description: Address 7 issues found in semantic review: SFU fire-and-forget race, upload filename extension sanitization, planner JSON validation, memory store logging, override ranges, Matrix bridge reconnection, and agent tools validation comment.
steps: 1. Fix SFU fire-and-forget race - Make createTransport and connectTransport async, await LiveKit operations, propagate errors properly instead of swallowing them.
2. Fix upload filename extension - Whitelist allowed extensions and strip dangerous ones. Store original filename as metadata.
3. Fix planner JSON parse - Add try/catch around JSON.parse with warning logging, validate the parsed shape before using it, fall back to keyword planning on validation failure.
4. Fix memory store silent failures - Log warnings when embedding generation fails while still storing the memory for keyword search.
5. Fix override ranges - Change from open-ended >= to caret ^ ranges for vite and form-data.
6. Add Matrix bridge reconnection - Track connected state separately from started, handle sync.error and error events, schedule reconnection with exponential backoff.
7. Add agent tools comment - Document that input validation is intentionally server-side.
acceptance_criteria: SFU createTransport and connectTransport await LiveKit operations and propagate errors
Upload service only allows whitelisted file extensions in S3 keys
Planner logs warning and falls back to keyword planning on invalid LLM JSON
Memory store logs warning when embedding generation fails
Override ranges use ^ instead of >= for vite and form-data
Matrix bridge handles disconnections and attempts reconnection with backoff
Agent tools have comment explaining intentional server-side validation
All existing tests pass with updates for new async signatures
findings: The SFU tests needed to be updated to await the now-async createTransport and connectTransport. Added 2 new test cases for error propagation (room creation failure, token generation failure). The pnpm-lock.yaml was updated when changing the override ranges. Used @quant/common logger instead of pino since it was already a dependency of @quant/agentic. All 4 packages pass typecheck, lint, and tests (156 agentic + 173 quantmeet + 103 quantdrive + federation).
